summary: This session provided an overview of traditional banking, covering its history, services, customer acquisition strategies, marketing, pricing, risk management, regulations, technology, payment processing, security, and customer service. Key topics included the importance of operational efficiency and the role of technology in enhancing banking services.
facts: Traditional banking dates back to ancient Mesopotamia around 2000 BC. Traditional banks offer services such as checking and savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and credit cards. Customer acquisition strategies for traditional banks include advertising, referral programs, and direct mail campaigns. Traditional banks use tiered pricing and bundling strategies to attract and retain customers. Risk management practices in traditional banks include assessing creditworthiness and monitoring loan portfolios. Banks must comply with regulations related to safety, consumer protection, and anti-money laundering. Financial statements for banks include income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements. Traditional banks utilize online and mobile banking, ATMs, and payment processing systems to provide services. Payment processing includes ACH systems, wire transfers, and debit and credit card systems. Security measures in traditional banks include encryption, firewalls, and fraud detection tools. Mobile banking apps allow customers to perform banking activities using smartphones or tablets. Internal control systems in banks include segregation of duties and dual control to prevent fraud. Operational efficiency is achieved through technology and automation in traditional banks. Customer service channels for banks include in-person service, telephone support, email support, and online chat support.
